mechanism_narrative: Sustained GDP growth, as measured by quarterly GDPC1, drives corporate earnings expectations higher and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. This is a persistent secular trend signal.
failure_condition: If trailing four-quarter GDP growth is above 2% but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances over any rolling 5-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above the 5% threshold signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate profit expectations, which depresses equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: If triggered events produce positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous increase in both the federal funds rate and the unemployment rate indicates a stagflationary environment where monetary tightening fails to control inflation while growth stalls, causing equity markets to decline. The joint signal is stronger than either component alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months and UNRATE > 4.5%
failure_condition: If the interaction term (FEDFUNDS * UNRATE) has a rank IC below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or if joint increase periods show average 20-day return > 0%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, strong retail sales growth signals overheating that prompts further policy tightening, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuations. The negative effect is amplified when the central bank is actively raising rates.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day S&P 500 return during tightening cycles with positive retail sales surprises is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.1), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity and supply-side health, which translates into positive short-term equity momentum as investors adjust to improving economic conditions.
failure_condition: If the correlation between monthly INDPRO growth and 5-day S&P 500 forward returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.5 million units annually, it signals a robust housing market and construction sector, boosting economic confidence and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If the proportion of positive 60-day returns following trigger events is less than 60% over any 5-year evaluation window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in payroll employment and industrial production indicates a broad-based economic expansion with both labor market strength and manufacturing output, which strongly supports equity market gains. The joint positive signal reinforces confidence in sustainable growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0 and INDPRO month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: If joint positive months produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 30% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (negative monthly change) signals an impending economic slowdown or recession, leading to negative equity returns over the following week as investors de-risk.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day S&P 500 return following INDPRO contraction months is positive or not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), the hypothesis is falsified.
